sections.description: AI-powered CSS/JS asset scanner for WordPress.
sections.changelog: Version 1.7.79b (beta): the ET Result Ratchet's "↩ +N" recovered-rules badge on the scan results table counted per-device rule legs instead of distinct rules, so a rule restored on both desktop and mobile double-counted — the badge could exceed a page's Aggressive (A) count (e.g. A:20 shown "↩ +39"). It now counts distinct restored rules (a per-pattern set of recollapse keys url_pattern|handle|type|group_id), so a both-device restore counts once. The ratchet's actual restoration behavior (demotion-aware union) is unchanged — only the displayed count's domain was wrong. The badge is also restyled (muted grey, one font-size smaller) so it isn't mistaken for the S/A/N counts.
